env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  checks:
    name: Check scope-worker
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
        with:
          toolchain: 1.94.0
          components: rustfmt, clippy

      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/git
            ~/.cargo/registry
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-worker-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-worker-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-worker-cargo-

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

      - name: Test
        run: cargo test -p scope-core -p worker --locked

      - name: Lint
        run: cargo clippy -p scope-core -p worker --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings

  deploy:
    name: Deploy scope-worker
    needs: checks
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Stage Railway upload root
        shell: bash
        run: |
          rm -rf .railway-upload
          mkdir -p .railway-upload
          cp Cargo.toml Cargo.lock rust-toolchain.toml .railway-upload/
          cp worker/railpack.json .railway-upload/railpack.json
          cp -a api .railway-upload/api
          cp -a worker .railway-upload/worker
          printf '%s\n' "${GITHUB_SHA:-manual}-${GITHUB_RUN_ID:-local}-${GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT:-0}" > .railway-upload/worker/railway-deploy-ref.txt
          cp -a crates .railway-upload/crates

      - name: Install Railway CLI
        run: npm install -g @railway/cli@5.23.1

      - name: Deploy to Railway
        shell: bash
        env:
          RAILWAY_DEPLOY_MESSAGE: ${{ github.event.head_commit.message || github.workflow }}
          RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID }}
          RAILWAY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_TOKEN }}
        run: bash .github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh scope-worker .railway-upload
      - name: Lint
        run: cargo clippy --manifest-path cli/Cargo.toml --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings

      - name: Build release binaries
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: >
          cargo build
          --manifest-path cli/Cargo.toml
          --release
          --locked
          --bin scope
          --bin scope-cli-service

  build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.label }}
    needs: checks
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - label: Linux x64
            runner: ubuntu-latest
            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            artifact: scope-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            binary: target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/scope
            builder: cargo
            smoke: true
          - label: Linux ARM64
            runner: ubuntu-latest
            target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            artifact: scope-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            binary: target/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/scope
            builder: cross
            smoke: false
          - label: macOS Intel
            runner: macos-15-intel
            target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            artifact: scope-x86_64-apple-darwin
            binary: target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/scope
            builder: cargo
            smoke: true
          - label: macOS Apple Silicon
            runner: macos-15
            target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            artifact: scope-aarch64-apple-darwin
            binary: target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/scope
            builder: cargo
            smoke: true
          - label: Windows x64
            runner: windows-latest
            target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            artifact: scope-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.exe
            binary: target/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/release/scope.exe
            builder: cargo
            smoke: true
          - label: Windows ARM64
            runner: windows-latest
            target: aarch64-pc-windows-msvc
            artifact: scope-aarch64-pc-windows-msvc.exe
            binary: target/aarch64-pc-windows-msvc/release/scope.exe
            builder: cargo
            smoke: false

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
        with:
          toolchain: 1.94.0
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/git
            ~/.cargo/registry
            cli/target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('cli/Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('cli/Cargo.lock') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-

      - name: Install cross
        if: matrix.builder == 'cross'
        working-directory: cli
        env:
          CARGO_TARGET_DIR: target/cargo-install
        run: cargo install cross --locked

      - name: Build scope
        working-directory: cli
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [ "${{ matrix.builder }}" = "cross" ]; then
            cross build --release --locked --target "${{ matrix.target }}" --bin scope
          else
            cargo build --release --locked --target "${{ matrix.target }}" --bin scope
          fi

      - name: Smoke test
        if: matrix.smoke
        working-directory: cli
        shell: bash
        run: ./${{ matrix.binary }} --help

      - name: Stage artifact
        working-directory: cli
        shell: bash
        run: |
          mkdir -p dist
          cp "${{ matrix.binary }}" "dist/${{ matrix.artifact }}"

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
          path: cli/dist/${{ matrix.artifact }}
          if-no-files-found: error

  deploy:
    name: Deploy scope-cli
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Download artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          path: cli/dist
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Verify artifacts and write checksums
        shell: bash
        run: |
          expected=(
            scope-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            scope-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            scope-x86_64-apple-darwin

Dependency order

Shrink first, then straighten boundaries.

Dead code is removed before persistence and domain boundaries are rebuilt, so later work does not preserve or migrate unreachable concepts. Behavior locks are established before any deletion.

Phase 0Capture behavior and count baseline
Phase 1Delete dead vertical slices
Phase 2Collapse persistence
Phase 3Centralize domain and contracts
Phase 4Simplify side effects, Git, and CLI
Phase 5Remove competing web state
Phase 6Consolidate tests and tooling
Phase Delete Add Expected net
0. Behavior locks0–1000–200+0 to +200
1. Dead surfaces1,250–1,85050–150−1,100 to −1,800
2. Persistence collapse4,800–6,5001,300–2,000−3,500 to −4,500
3. Domain and contracts1,200–1,700700–1,000−500 to −900

6. Tests and tooling1,200–1,700450–700−700 to −1,000
Campaign total10,650–15,0503,250–5,300−7,000 to −10,300

Delete/add ranges are paired estimates within each phase; crossing the most conservative gross endpoints does not produce the expected net. The planning target is 8,000–10,000 net lines. The lower bound absorbs replacement code and behavior-lock tests. Falling below 7,000 requires an explicit explanation and a new approved plan.

Deleting behavior disguised as dead code Prove no production constructor/caller, then retain supported behavior characterization tests. Memory/Postgres semantic drift during removal Make Postgres tests authoritative before deleting memory branches; compare ordering and error behavior. Async conversion spreads too broadly Convert one persistence behavior family at a time; adapters await directly without sync wrappers. Authorization drift Decision-table tests precede consolidation; API concealment tests remain at the boundary. LOC pressure weakens readability Reject minification, giant files, hidden generated logic, or abstractions whose only purpose is reducing count. Schema incompatibility Use explicit destructive pre-alpha reset; do not add compatibility migrations.
Each phase should land independently. If a phase changes supported behavior, stop and ask the owner rather than carrying an unapproved change into later refactors. Rollback is phase-level revert plus disposable database reset.

Preserve

Good foundations stay.

Pattern Decision
RepoMutation / RepoEffects Keep Expand the explicit-side-effect pattern to notifications and cleanup receipts.
Generated Rust → TypeScript contract check Keep Extend it to the CLI/shared contract and SSE payload.
Durable cleanup/outbox rows Keep Repair claiming, retry, and ownership rather than returning to fire-and-forget work.
Git subprocess safety Keep Preserve bounded stderr, timeout, process-group termination, wait, and redaction.
Pure file-tree and diff parsing models Keep They already have focused ownership and tests.
Cross-platform CLI distribution Keep Consolidate metadata; do not reduce supported targets.

const patch = "*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/package.json
@@
     "start": "node .output/server/index.mjs",
     "test": "node -e \"const fs=require('node:fs'); fs.rmSync('.test-output',{recursive:true,force:true}); fs.mkdirSync('.test-output',{recursive:true}); fs.writeFileSync('.test-output/package.json','{\\\"type\\\":\\\"commonjs\\\"}')\" && tsc -p tsconfig.test.json && node --test \".test-output/**/*.test.js\"",
+    "test:smoke": "node --test \"smoke/**/*.spec.mjs\"",
     "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
*** Add File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/smoke/public-repository.spec.mjs
+import assert from 'node:assert/strict'
+import { after, before, test } from 'node:test'
+import { chromium } from 'playwright'
+
+const baseUrl = (
+  process.env.SCOPE_WEB_BASE_URL ??
+  process.env.PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL ??
+  'http://localhost:3000'
+).replace(/\/$/, '')
+const repoId = process.env.SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO ?? process.env.UI_AUDIT_REPO ?? 'dev/public-demo'
+const [owner, repo, extra] = repoId.split('/')
+
+if (!owner || !repo || extra) {
+  throw new Error('SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO must be an owner/repository pair')
+}
+
+const repoPath = `/repos/${encodeURIComponent(owner)}/${encodeURIComponent(repo)}`
+let browser
+
+before(async () => {
+  browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true })
+})
+
+after(async () => {
+  await browser?.close()
+})
+
+test('public repository exposes only its projected source', async () => {
+  await withPage(repoPath, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: repoId }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'Code')
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 2, name: 'Source' }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('README.md', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('app.ts', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+    assert.equal(await page.getByText('internal', { exact: true }).count(), 0)
+    assert.equal(await page.getByText('plan.md', { exact: true }).count(), 0)
+  })
+})
+
+test('public repository history renders its seeded commit', async () => {
+  await withPage(`${repoPath}/history`, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: 'History' }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'History')
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 2, name: 'Commits' }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('Seed public demo', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+  })
+})
+
+test('public repository requests route is anonymously readable', async () => {
+  await withPage(`${repoPath}/requests`, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: 'Requests' }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'Requests')
+    await page.getByText('No requests yet', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+  })
+})
+
+async function withPage(path, assertion) {
+  const page = await browser.newPage()
+  const pageErrors = []
+  page.on('pageerror', (error) => pageErrors.push(error.message))
+
+  try {
+    const response = await page.goto(new URL(path, `${baseUrl}/`).toString(), {
+      timeout: 30_000,
+      waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',
+    })
+    assert(response, `navigation to ${path} did not produce a response`)
+    assert(response.status() < 400, `navigation to ${path} returned ${response.status()}`)
+    await assertion(page)
+    assert.deepEqual(pageErrors, [])
+  } finally {
+    await page.close()
+  }
+}

`dev/scope-dev` is the supported local development entrypoint.
`dev/api` contains Rust code that is compiled only for `--features local-dev`;
it is intentionally outside `api/src` so it is not part of the production API
runtime.

```bash
./dev/scope-dev doctor
./dev/scope-dev up
./dev/scope-dev bench
./dev/scope-dev status
./dev/scope-dev down
./dev/scope-dev reset
```

The command starts:

- web at `http://localhost:3000`
- API at `http://localhost:8080`
- worker

Local API runs with `cargo run -p api --features local-dev`, Postgres metadata
seeded with demo repositories, and filesystem object storage under `.scope/dev`.
Set a safe local `DATABASE_URL` in root `.env.local`. The script strips inherited
Railway variables and refuses production-looking Clerk or database settings.

`web/.env.local` must contain Clerk development keys. The script derives
`CLERK_ISSUER` for the local API from `VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`.

Root `.env.local` must contain `SCOPE_DEV_USER_EMAIL` matching the Clerk dev
account you sign in with. `SCOPE_DEV_USER_HANDLE` is optional.

The local dev stack intentionally does not start the CLI installer service.
Seeded repositories are the default UI development path until a separate CLI dev
environment exists.

Run `./dev/scope-dev bench` while the stack is up to collect the Phase 0 local
data-architecture baseline. The benchmark harness lives under `bench/`, uses
the seeded local repos, reuses the local Scope CLI session when available, and
writes ignored reports to `.tmp/bench/phase0/`.

+import assert from 'node:assert/strict'
+import { after, before, test } from 'node:test'
+import { chromium } from 'playwright'
+
+const baseUrl = (
+  process.env.SCOPE_WEB_BASE_URL ??
+  process.env.PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL ??
+  'http://localhost:3000'
+).replace(/\/$/, '')
+const repoId = process.env.SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO ?? process.env.UI_AUDIT_REPO ?? 'dev/public-demo'
+const [owner, repo, extra] = repoId.split('/')
+
+if (!owner || !repo || extra) {
+  throw new Error('SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO must be an owner/repository pair')
+}
+
+const repoPath = `/repos/${encodeURIComponent(owner)}/${encodeURIComponent(repo)}`
+let browser
+
+before(async () => {
+  browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true })
+})
+
+after(async () => {
+  await browser?.close()
+})
+
+test('public repository exposes only its projected source', async () => {
+  await withPage(repoPath, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: repoId }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'Code')
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 2, name: 'Source' }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('README.md', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('app.ts', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+    assert.equal(await page.getByText('internal', { exact: true }).count(), 0)
+    assert.equal(await page.getByText('plan.md', { exact: true }).count(), 0)
+  })
+})
+
+test('public repository history renders its seeded commit', async () => {
+  await withPage(`${repoPath}/history`, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: 'History' }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'History')
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 2, name: 'Commits' }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('Seed public demo', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+  })
+})
+
+test('public repository requests route is anonymously readable', async () => {
+  await withPage(`${repoPath}/requests`, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: 'Requests' }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'Requests')
+    await page.getByText('No requests yet', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+  })
+})
+
+async function withPage(path, assertion) {
+  const page = await browser.newPage()
+  const pageErrors = []
+  page.on('pageerror', (error) => pageErrors.push(error.message))
+
+  try {
+    const response = await page.goto(new URL(path, `${baseUrl}/`).toString(), {
+      timeout: 30_000,
+      waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',
+    })
+    assert(response, `navigation to ${path} did not produce a response`)
+    assert(response.status() < 400, `navigation to ${path} returned ${response.status()}`)
+    await assertion(page)
+    assert.deepEqual(pageErrors, [])
+  } finally {
+    await page.close()
+  }
+}
+
+async function assertCurrentRepoSection(page, section) {
+  const link = page
+    .getByRole('navigation', { name: 'Repository' })
+    .getByRole('link', { name: section, exact: true })
+  await link.waitFor()

+}

---
name: systematic-debugging
description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
---

# Systematic Debugging

## Overview

Random fixes waste time and create new bugs. Quick patches mask underlying issues.

**Core principle:** ALWAYS find root cause before attempting fixes. Symptom fixes are failure.

**Violating the letter of this process is violating the spirit of debugging.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST
```

If you haven't completed Phase 1, you cannot propose fixes.

## When to Use

Use for ANY technical issue:
- Test failures
- Bugs in production
- Unexpected behavior
- Performance problems
- Build failures
- Integration issues

**Use this ESPECIALLY when:**
- Under time pressure (emergencies make guessing tempting)
- "Just one quick fix" seems obvious
- You've already tried multiple fixes
- Previous fix didn't work
- You don't fully understand the issue

**Don't skip when:**
- Issue seems simple (simple bugs have root causes too)
- You're in a hurry (rushing guarantees rework)
- Manager wants it fixed NOW (systematic is faster than thrashing)

## The Four Phases

You MUST complete each phase before proceeding to the next.

### Phase 1: Root Cause Investigation

**BEFORE attempting ANY fix:**

1. **Read Error Messages Carefully**
   - Don't skip past errors or warnings
   - They often contain the exact solution
   - Read stack traces completely
   - Note line numbers, file paths, error codes

2. **Reproduce Consistently**
   - Can you trigger it reliably?
   - What are the exact steps?
   - Does it happen every time?
   - If not reproducible → gather more data, don't guess

3. **Check Recent Changes**
   - What changed that could cause this?
   - Git diff, recent commits
   - New dependencies, config changes
   - Environmental differences

4. **Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems**

   **WHEN system has multiple components (CI → build → signing, API → service → database):**

   **BEFORE proposing fixes, add diagnostic instrumentation:**
   ```
   For EACH component boundary:
     - Log what data enters component
     - Log what data exits component
     - Verify environment/config propagation
     - Check state at each layer

   Run once to gather evidence showing WHERE it breaks
   THEN analyze evidence to identify failing component
   THEN investigate that specific component
   ```

   **Example (multi-layer system):**
   ```bash
   # Layer 1: Workflow
   echo "=== Secrets available in workflow: ==="
   echo "IDENTITY: ${IDENTITY:+SET}${IDENTITY:-UNSET}"

   # Layer 2: Build script
   echo "=== Env vars in build script: ==="
   env | grep IDENTITY || echo "IDENTITY not in environment"

   # Layer 3: Signing script
   echo "=== Keychain state: ==="
   security list-keychains
   security find-identity -v

   # Layer 4: Actual signing
   codesign --sign "$IDENTITY" --verbose=4 "$APP"
   ```

   **This reveals:** Which layer fails (secrets → workflow ✓, workflow → build ✗)

5. **Trace Data Flow**

   **WHEN error is deep in call stack:**

   See `root-cause-tracing.md` in this directory for the complete backward tracing technique.

   **Quick version:**
   - Where does bad value originate?
   - What called this with bad value?
   - Keep tracing up until you find the source
   - Fix at source, not at symptom

### Phase 2: Pattern Analysis

**Find the pattern before fixing:**

1. **Find Working Examples**
   - Locate similar working code in same codebase
   - What works that's similar to what's broken?

2. **Compare Against References**
   - If implementing pattern, read reference implementation COMPLETELY
   - Don't skim - read every line
   - Understand the pattern fully before applying

3. **Identify Differences**
   - What's different between working and broken?
   - List every difference, however small
   - Don't assume "that can't matter"

4. **Understand Dependencies**
   - What other components does this need?
   - What settings, config, environment?
   - What assumptions does it make?

### Phase 3: Hypothesis and Testing

**Scientific method:**

1. **Form Single Hypothesis**
   - State clearly: "I think X is the root cause because Y"
   - Write it down
   - Be specific, not vague

2. **Test Minimally**
   - Make the SMALLEST possible change to test hypothesis
   - One variable at a time
   - Don't fix multiple things at once

3. **Verify Before Continuing**
   - Did it work? Yes → Phase 4
   - Didn't work? Form NEW hypothesis
   - DON'T add more fixes on top

4. **When You Don't Know**
   - Say "I don't understand X"
   - Don't pretend to know
   - Ask for help
   - Research more

### Phase 4: Implementation

**Fix the root cause, not the symptom:**

1. **Create Failing Test Case**
   - Simplest possible reproduction
   - Automated test if possible
   - One-off test script if no framework
   - MUST have before fixing
   - Use the `superpowers:test-driven-development` skill for writing proper failing tests

2. **Implement Single Fix**
   - Address the root cause identified
   - ONE change at a time
   - No "while I'm here" improvements
   - No bundled refactoring

3. **Verify Fix**
   - Test passes now?
   - No other tests broken?
   - Issue actually resolved?

4. **If Fix Doesn't Work**
   - STOP
   - Count: How many fixes have you tried?
   - If < 3: Return to Phase 1, re-analyze with new information
   - **If ≥ 3: STOP and question the architecture (step 5 below)**
   - DON'T attempt Fix #4 without architectural discussion

5. **If 3+ Fixes Failed: Question Architecture**

   **Pattern indicating architectural problem:**
   - Each fix reveals new shared state/coupling/problem in different place
   - Fixes require "massive refactoring" to implement
   - Each fix creates new symptoms elsewhere

   **STOP and question fundamentals:**
   - Is this pattern fundamentally sound?
   - Are we "sticking with it through sheer inertia"?
   - Should we refactor architecture vs. continue fixing symptoms?

   **Discuss with your human partner before attempting more fixes**

   This is NOT a failed hypothesis - this is a wrong architecture.

## Red Flags - STOP and Follow Process

If you catch yourself thinking:
- "Quick fix for now, investigate later"
- "Just try changing X and see if it works"
- "Add multiple changes, run tests"
- "Skip the test, I'll manually verify"
- "It's probably X, let me fix that"
- "I don't fully understand but this might work"
- "Pattern says X but I'll adapt it differently"
- "Here are the main problems: [lists fixes without investigation]"
- Proposing solutions before tracing data flow
- **"One more fix attempt" (when already tried 2+)**
- **Each fix reveals new problem in different place**

**ALL of these mean: STOP. Return to Phase 1.**

**If 3+ fixes failed:** Question the architecture (see Phase 4.5)

## your human partner's Signals You're Doing It Wrong

**Watch for these redirections:**
- "Is that not happening?" - You assumed without verifying
- "Will it show us...?" - You should have added evidence gathering
- "Stop guessing" - You're proposing fixes without understanding
- "Ultrathink this" - Question fundamentals, not just symptoms
- "We're stuck?" (frustrated) - Your approach isn't working

**When you see these:** STOP. Return to Phase 1.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Issue is simple, don't need process" | Simple issues have root causes too. Process is fast for simple bugs. |
| "Emergency, no time for process" | Systematic debugging is FASTER than guess-and-check thrashing. |
| "Just try this first, then investigate" | First fix sets the pattern. Do it right from the start. |
| "I'll write test after confirming fix works" | Untested fixes don't stick. Test first proves it. |
| "Multiple fixes at once saves time" | Can't isolate what worked. Causes new bugs. |
| "Reference too long, I'll adapt the pattern" | Partial understanding guarantees bugs. Read it completely. |
| "I see the problem, let me fix it" | Seeing symptoms ≠ understanding root cause. |
| "One more fix attempt" (after 2+ failures) | 3+ failures = architectural problem. Question pattern, don't fix again. |

## Quick Reference

| Phase | Key Activities | Success Criteria |
|-------|---------------|------------------|
| **1. Root Cause** | Read errors, reproduce, check changes, gather evidence | Understand WHAT and WHY |
| **2. Pattern** | Find working examples, compare | Identify differences |
| **3. Hypothesis** | Form theory, test minimally | Confirmed or new hypothesis |
| **4. Implementation** | Create test, fix, verify | Bug resolved, tests pass |

## When Process Reveals "No Root Cause"

If systematic investigation reveals issue is truly environmental, timing-dependent, or external:

1. You've completed the process
2. Document what you investigated
3. Implement appropriate handling (retry, timeout, error message)
4. Add monitoring/logging for future investigation

**But:** 95% of "no root cause" cases are incomplete investigation.

## Supporting Techniques

These techniques are part of systematic debugging and available in this directory:

- **`root-cause-tracing.md`** - Trace bugs backward through call stack to find original trigger
- **`defense-in-depth.md`** - Add validation at multiple layers after finding root cause
- **`condition-based-waiting.md`** - Replace arbitrary timeouts with condition polling

**Related skills:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - For creating failing test case (Phase 4, Step 1)
- **superpowers:verification-before-completion** - Verify fix worked before claiming success

## Real-World Impact

From debugging sessions:
- Systematic approach: 15-30 minutes to fix
- Random fixes approach: 2-3 hours of thrashing
- First-time fix rate: 95% vs 40%
- New bugs introduced: Near zero vs common

payload.message: A dry-list check exposed one test-harness flaw: a file-level browser hook can hang when Node filters out every test, because setup runs without a matching teardown path. I’m switching to per-test browser ownership so filtered runs and individual failures always clean up their process.

content.text: A dry-list check exposed one test-harness flaw: a file-level browser hook can hang when Node filters out every test, because setup runs without a matching teardown path. I’m switching to per-test browser ownership so filtered runs and individual failures always clean up their process.

payload.message: The focused run found two assertion assumptions, not application failures: the source tree intentionally collapses `src`, so `app.ts` is not initially visible, and public projection intentionally sanitizes the logical commit message to “Projected public update.” I’m updating the smoke checks to assert stable public behavior—file count, visible README/private-path exclusion, projected commit ID and sanitized label.

content.text: The focused run found two assertion assumptions, not application failures: the source tree intentionally collapses `src`, so `app.ts` is not initially visible, and public projection intentionally sanitizes the logical commit message to “Projected public update.” I’m updating the smoke checks to assert stable public behavior—file count, visible README/private-path exclusion, projected commit ID and sanitized label.

payload.message:
Implemented and verified the minimal Playwright smoke layer.

Changes:

name: Scope API CI

on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - ".github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh"
      - ".github/workflows/scope-api-ci.yml"
      - "Cargo.lock"
      - "Cargo.toml"
      - "api/**"
      - "crates/scope-core/**"
      - "worker/**"
      - "web/scripts/check-api-contract.mjs"
      - "web/src/api/types.generated.ts"
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    paths:
      - ".github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh"
      - ".github/workflows/scope-api-ci.yml"
      - "Cargo.lock"
      - "Cargo.toml"
      - "api/**"
      - "crates/scope-core/**"
      - "web/scripts/check-api-contract.mjs"
      - "web/src/api/types.generated.ts"
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true
  SCOPE_TEST_DATABASE_URL: REDACTED

jobs:
  checks:
    name: Check scope-api
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: scope_test
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: scope
          POSTGRES_USER: scope
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U scope -d scope_test"
          --health-interval 5s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 10
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
        with:
          toolchain: 1.94.0
          components: rustfmt, clippy

      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/git
            ~/.cargo/registry
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-api-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-api-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-api-cargo-

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

      - name: Test
        run: cargo test -p scope-core -p api -p worker --locked

      - name: Lint
        run: cargo clippy -p scope-core -p api -p worker --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings

      - name: Install Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Check API TypeScript contract
        working-directory: web
        run: node scripts/check-api-contract.mjs

  deploy:
    name: Deploy scope-api
    needs: checks
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Stage Railway upload root
        shell: bash
        run: |
          rm -rf .railway-upload
          mkdir -p .railway-upload
          cp Cargo.toml Cargo.lock rust-toolchain.toml .railway-upload/
          cp api/railpack.json .railway-upload/railpack.json
          cp -a api .railway-upload/api
          printf '%s\n' "${GITHUB_SHA:-manual}-${GITHUB_RUN_ID:-local}-${GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT:-0}" > .railway-upload/api/railway-deploy-ref.txt
          cp -a worker .railway-upload/worker
          cp -a crates .railway-upload/crates

      - name: Install Railway CLI
name: Scope Worker CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    paths:
      - ".github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh"
      - ".github/workflows/scope-worker-ci.yml"
      - "Cargo.lock"
      - "Cargo.toml"
      - "crates/scope-core/**"
      - "worker/**"
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true
  SCOPE_TEST_DATABASE_URL: REDACTED

jobs:
  checks:
    name: Check scope-worker
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: scope_test
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: scope
          POSTGRES_USER: scope
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U scope -d scope_test"
          --health-interval 5s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 10
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
        with:
          toolchain: 1.94.0
          components: rustfmt, clippy

      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/git
            ~/.cargo/registry
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-worker-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-worker-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-worker-cargo-

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

      - name: Test
        run: cargo test -p scope-core -p worker --locked

      - name: Lint
        run: cargo clippy -p scope-core -p worker --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings

  deploy:
    name: Deploy scope-worker
    needs: checks
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Stage Railway upload root
        shell: bash
        run: |
          rm -rf .railway-upload
          mkdir -p .railway-upload
          cp Cargo.toml Cargo.lock rust-toolchain.toml .railway-upload/
          cp worker/railpack.json .railway-upload/railpack.json
          cp -a api .railway-upload/api
          cp -a worker .railway-upload/worker
          printf '%s\n' "${GITHUB_SHA:-manual}-${GITHUB_RUN_ID:-local}-${GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT:-0}" > .railway-upload/worker/railway-deploy-ref.txt
          cp -a crates .railway-upload/crates

      - name: Install Railway CLI
        run: npm install -g @railway/cli@5.23.1

      - name: Deploy to Railway
        shell: bash
        env:
          RAILWAY_DEPLOY_MESSAGE: ${{ github.event.head_commit.message || github.workflow }}
          RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID }}

payload.message: The CI duplication has a safe consolidation point: both jobs use the same Postgres service, toolchain, cache, format/test/clippy sequence, and only differ by whether API and contract checks are included. I’m replacing those copied jobs with one local reusable workflow controlled by a boolean, leaving each deployment workflow and its trigger semantics intact.

name: Rust Workspace Checks

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      cache-scope:
        required: true
        type: string
      include-api:
        default: false
        required: false
        type: boolean

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true
  SCOPE_TEST_DATABASE_URL: REDACTED

jobs:
  checks:
    name: Check scope-${{ inputs.cache-scope }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: scope_test
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: scope
          POSTGRES_USER: scope
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U scope -d scope_test"
          --health-interval 5s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 10
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
        with:
          toolchain: 1.94.0
          components: rustfmt, clippy

      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/git
            ~/.cargo/registry
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ inputs.cache-scope }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ inputs.cache-scope }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ inputs.cache-scope }}-cargo-

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

      - name: Test API workspace
        if: inputs.include-api
        run: cargo test -p scope-core -p api -p worker --locked

      - name: Test worker workspace
        if: ${{ !inputs.include-api }}
        run: cargo test -p scope-core -p worker --locked

      - name: Lint API workspace
        if: inputs.include-api
        run: cargo clippy -p scope-core -p api -p worker --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings

      - name: Lint worker workspace
        if: ${{ !inputs.include-api }}
        run: cargo clippy -p scope-core -p worker --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings

      - name: Install Node
        if: inputs.include-api
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Check API TypeScript contract
        if: inputs.include-api
        working-directory: web
        run: node scripts/check-api-contract.mjs

name: Scope API CI

on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - ".github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh"
      - ".github/workflows/scope-api-ci.yml"
      - ".github/workflows/rust-workspace-checks.yml"
      - "Cargo.lock"
      - "Cargo.toml"
      - "api/**"
      - "crates/scope-core/**"
      - "worker/**"
      - "web/scripts/check-api-contract.mjs"
      - "web/src/api/types.generated.ts"
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    paths:
      - ".github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh"
      - ".github/workflows/scope-api-ci.yml"
      - ".github/workflows/rust-workspace-checks.yml"
      - "Cargo.lock"
      - "Cargo.toml"
      - "api/**"
      - "crates/scope-core/**"
      - "web/scripts/check-api-contract.mjs"
      - "web/src/api/types.generated.ts"
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  checks:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/rust-workspace-checks.yml
    with:
      cache-scope: api
      include-api: true

  deploy:
    name: Deploy scope-api
    needs: checks
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    env:
      FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Stage Railway upload root
        shell: bash
        run: |
          rm -rf .railway-upload
          mkdir -p .railway-upload
          cp Cargo.toml Cargo.lock rust-toolchain.toml .railway-upload/
          cp api/railpack.json .railway-upload/railpack.json
          cp -a api .railway-upload/api
          printf '%s\n' "${GITHUB_SHA:-manual}-${GITHUB_RUN_ID:-local}-${GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT:-0}" > .railway-upload/api/railway-deploy-ref.txt
          cp -a worker .railway-upload/worker
          cp -a crates .railway-upload/crates

      - name: Install Railway CLI
        run: npm install -g @railway/cli@5.23.1

      - name: Deploy to Railway
        shell: bash
        env:
          RAILWAY_DEPLOY_MESSAGE: ${{ github.event.head_commit.message || github.workflow }}
          RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID }}
          RAILWAY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_TOKEN }}
        run: bash .github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh scope-api .railway-upload
name: Scope Worker CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    paths:
      - ".github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh"
      - ".github/workflows/scope-worker-ci.yml"
      - ".github/workflows/rust-workspace-checks.yml"
      - "Cargo.lock"
      - "Cargo.toml"
      - "crates/scope-core/**"
      - "worker/**"
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

permissions:

name: Rust Workspace Checks

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      include-api:
        default: false
        required: false
        type: boolean

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true
  SCOPE_TEST_DATABASE_URL: REDACTED

jobs:
  checks:
    name: Check Rust workspace
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: scope_test
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: scope
          POSTGRES_USER: scope
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U scope -d scope_test"
          --health-interval 5s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 10
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
        with:
          toolchain: 1.94.0
          components: rustfmt, clippy

      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/git
            ~/.cargo/registry
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-rust-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-rust-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-rust-cargo-

      - name: Check Rust workspace
        shell: bash
        run: |
          packages=(-p scope-core -p worker)
          if [[ "${{ inputs.include-api }}" == "true" ]]; then
            packages+=(-p api)
          fi
          cargo fmt --all --check
          cargo test "${packages[@]}" --locked
          cargo clippy "${packages[@]}" --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings

      - name: Install Node
        if: inputs.include-api
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Check API TypeScript contract
        if: inputs.include-api
        working-directory: web
        run: node scripts/check-api-contract.mjs
